cell.rationale: A single clear offence clause governs a hand-scored goal, with no competing clause.
The act was admitted by the scorer, so the deliberateness residual is resolved.
No review technology existed and the call was unreviewable in the moment (hist-tech-1986).
Named sources frame the same agreed handball in opposite valence — justification versus condemnation.

framing-maradona-1986.extracted_text: Diego Maradona (1986) — justifying: the goal was scored 'a little with the head of Maradona and a little with the hand of God'.

framing-shilton.extracted_text: Peter Shilton (England goalkeeper) — condemning: described the goal as cheating and said Maradona never apologised.

hist-tech-1986.attribution: Curated historical record — The Hand of God
hist-tech-1986.extracted_text: No video review system existed at the 1986 World Cup; the referee's decision in the moment was final and unreviewable.

ifab-law12-handball-offence-p110.extracted_text: It is an offence if a player deliberately touches the ball with their hand/arm, or scores in the opponents' goal immediately after the ball has touched their hand/arm, even if accidental. A goal scored in this way does not stand.

sb-hand-of-god-body-part.attribution: Data provided by StatsBomb Open Data (StatsBomb User Agreement).
sb-hand-of-god-body-part.extracted_text: In the event data this goal's body part is recorded as the catch-all label 'Other', while a comparable goal by the same player is recorded with a specific body-part class ('Left Foot'). The shot ontology has no category for a hand-scored goal, so the event falls back to 'Other'. This describes the data model only.

output.rationale: The retrieved Law states a goal scored from the hand or arm does not stand (ifab-law12-handball-offence-p110). The rule yields one outcome for a hand-scored goal, so it is clear; rule-ambiguity does not hold.

The aggregate records this goal's body part as the catch-all label 'Other' while a comparable goal is a specific class (sb-hand-of-god-body-part). The schema having no category for the action is a point where the data model strains. This describes the data model only.

The retrieved facts state no video review existed at the time and the decision was final in the moment (hist-tech-1986). The decisive truth was not available to the officials then, a decision-time deficit.

Maradona is reported to have justified the goal as 'the hand of God' (framing-maradona-1986), while Shilton is reported to have condemned it as cheating (framing-shilton). Two named people describe one agreed event in opposing terms.

settled_fact.statement: Diego Maradona scored with his hand against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final. The referee allowed the goal, Argentina won, and the result was never overturned. The handball itself is not in dispute.

cells.rationale: A single clear offence clause governs a hand-scored goal, with no competing clause.
The act was admitted by the scorer, so the deliberateness residual is resolved.
No review technology existed and the call was unreviewable in the moment (hist-tech-1986).
Named sources frame the same agreed handball in opposite valence — justification versus condemnation.

split.headline: It never resolved over what the referee could see and which nation was watching — not because the Law was unclear.
